Brandon Ingram's Pelicans tenure is off to a historic start in the scoring column, even as the team still seeks crooked numbers in the win column.

The 22-year-old forward was one of the trio of former Los Angeles Lakers sent to New Orleans in exchange for Anthony Davis, along with guards Josh Hart and Lonzo Ball, and over the first week of the season he's led his team in scoring and been ultra-efficient in the process.

Ingram has averaged 27.3 points per game, chipped in 9.5 rebounds and 4.8 assists, and shot 50% from 3-point range across New Orleans' first four games. Those games included an overtime loss to the Toronto Raptors and tight losses to the Dallas Mavericks and Houston Rockets before a blowout defeat by the Golden State Warriors.

Ingram is just the second player in NBA history to score at least 109 points and shoot over 50 percent from 3-point range in his first four games with a new team,
